title: Pokey Mom

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in opening credits
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific but predictable

jokes.text: My juice box! Ohh!
jokes.setup: Homer appears to be injured from getting up
jokes.punchline: His primary concern is his juice box
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's childish priorities revealed in crisis

jokes.text: They're unveiling a combination apron-smock. It's called a 'smapron.'
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why the Apron Expo is exciting
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous portmanteau 'smapron'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - earnest excitement about something absurd

jokes.text: Did you say 'smockron'? No, smapron.
jokes.punchline: Creates an even more ridiculous word 'smockron'
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of the portmanteau joke through Homer's confusion

jokes.text: Man, that was a good Apron Expo! I'm gonna wear my apron on the Fourth of July.
jokes.setup: Homer was reluctant to go to the Apron Expo
jokes.punchline: He's now completely enthusiastic about it
jokes.explanation: Standard reversal of expectations, functional character work

jokes.text: And this lead apron will keep me safe downstairs. [Chuckles] 'Grill Power.'
jokes.setup: Homer showing off his apron purchases
jokes.punchline: Thinks he needs radiation protection for grilling
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - misunderstanding practical safety equipment

jokes.text: I'm a little disappointed. There were too many aprons.
jokes.setup: Homer just praised the Apron Expo
jokes.punchline: Complains there were too many aprons at an apron expo
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - complaining about the main feature of the event

jokes.text: But all I want to do right now is hit the adjustable sack with a good juice box.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to relax after the expo
jokes.punchline: His childish way of saying 'go to bed with a drink'
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the juice box and Homer's childish language

jokes.text: Because when all is said and done- 'Prison rodeo... today'!
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by roadside sign
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific interruption, sets up plot naturally

jokes.text: The contestants you'll see today are actual prisoners... on a break from their telemarketing duties.
jokes.punchline: Reveals prisoners do telemarketing
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary on prison labor and annoying telemarketers

jokes.text: Well, don't feel too bad for him, folks. He's in here for erecting a nativity scene on city property. [All Boo]
jokes.punchline: His 'crime' is something harmless that makes crowd boo
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of church-state separation hysteria

jokes.text: There's so much evil in the world.
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest but misplaced moral concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge reaction - missing the irony while being sincere

jokes.text: Bob Dylan wrote a song to keep him in prison.
jokes.punchline: References Dylan's 'Hurricane' about wrongful imprisonment but reversed
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of famous protest song, rewards cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Go, bull! Toss his salad!
jokes.punchline: Innocent phrase with prison sexual connotation
jokes.explanation: Adult humor that works on multiple levels, though somewhat cheap

jokes.text: And it looks like old Tornado wants to caramelize his crème brûlée!
jokes.punchline: Even more elaborate food-sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Escalates the double entendre but gets a bit forced

jokes.text: Is my lipstick even? Go like this. [Popping]
jokes.setup: Rodeo clown trying to distract bull
jokes.punchline: Concerned about makeup while in mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about vain clown with wrong priorities

jokes.text: That's not gonna do it, Marge. You need something red.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to help distract the bull
jokes.punchline: Homer knows bullfighting basics but is watching from stands
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke, functional but not funny on its own

jokes.text: Now for a little calming blue.
jokes.setup: Homer threw red shirt to attract bull
jokes.punchline: Follows up with blue to 'calm' the bull
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - applying color psychology to angry bull

jokes.text: Hey, where's your blue shirt? I don't have a blue shirt.
jokes.setup: Homer wants blue shirt for bull
jokes.punchline: Marge doesn't have one, undermining his plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's flawed logic meeting reality

jokes.text: Maybe it's the tear gas. [Sniffles] Or maybe this was the best damn prison rodeo ever.
jokes.setup: Chaos and tear gas at rodeo
jokes.punchline: Homer still rates it positively despite disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding positives in obvious disaster

jokes.text: I can't complain. Nah, that's for the prisoners. You can complain all you want.
jokes.setup: Marge asks how his back feels
jokes.punchline: Homer makes literal distinction about who can complain
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'can't complain' that sets up Homer's actual complaining

jokes.text: Oh, God, my back! It hurts so much! And my job is so unfulfilling!
jokes.punchline: Immediately launches into complaints beyond just his back
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup, escalates beyond physical pain

jokes.text: And his fondness for the guitar.
jokes.setup: Marge praising artistic capture of Jimi Hendrix's passion
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly literal observation
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal-mindedness contrasted with Marge's art appreciation

jokes.text: They won't give us art supplies... so I had to paint it with pudding.
jokes.setup: Admiring the quality of Jack's painting
jokes.punchline: Reveals he used food as paint
jokes.explanation: Good prison humor - artistic talent working with limitations

jokes.text: He painted a unicorn in outer space. I'm asking you. What's it breathing?
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pedantic criticism of fantasy art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - missing the point with nitpicky logic

jokes.text: Air. Ain't no air in space. There's an air and space museum.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy says there's no air in space
jokes.punchline: Homer's flawed logic about the museum name
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of museum names as evidence

jokes.text: Let's get out of this gosh-forsaken heck-hole.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to leave the prison
jokes.punchline: Uses mild euphemisms for strong language
jokes.explanation: Homer trying to be considerate with language while still expressing frustration

jokes.text: They are coming back for us, aren't they?
jokes.punchline: Kids' growing realization they're abandoned
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons parental neglect joke with kids as afterthought

jokes.text: Well, there's a dull ache certainly. And overlaid on that is a club sandwich of pain. Only instead of bacon there's agony.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about Homer's back pain
jokes.punchline: Homer's elaborate food metaphor for pain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating metaphor that's both absurd and poetic

jokes.text: Marge, can I have a B.L.T?
jokes.setup: Just finished elaborate sandwich metaphor about pain
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants actual sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - metaphor makes him hungry

jokes.text: How would you feel if I did some volunteer work at the prison? First, I'd feel like having a B.L.T. - then proud of you.
jokes.setup: Marge asks Homer's opinion on volunteering
jokes.punchline: Homer still focused on the sandwich before answering
jokes.explanation: Continues the B.L.T. running gag while showing Homer's priorities

jokes.text: Ooh, art class. La-di-da.
jokes.setup: Marge announces she's there to teach art class
jokes.punchline: Guard's dismissive, mocking response
jokes.explanation: Basic character work for tough prison guard, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: This is Ike Pross. He's going to solitary. Ooh, solitary. La-di-da.
jokes.punchline: Uses same mocking tone for solitary confinement
jokes.explanation: Escalates the running gag by applying it to something serious

jokes.text: Welcome to 'Freeing the Artist Within.' Not literally, of course. [Giggling]
jokes.punchline: Clarifies she doesn't mean literal prison escape
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - innocent phrase with darker implication in prison context

jokes.text: But the prison cafeteria would only give me sauerkraut. [Inmates Groaning] That's depressing.
jokes.setup: Marge wanted to paint fresh fruit
jokes.punchline: Forced to paint depressing sauerkraut instead
jokes.explanation: Good visual and institutional limitations creating artistic constraint

jokes.text: Let's let a little sunshine in. [Inmates Groaning]
jokes.setup: Marge trying to be cheerful in prison art class
jokes.punchline: Inmates groan at her optimistic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Marge's relentless positivity clashing with prison reality

jokes.text: Can I smell your dress? [All Laugh] Hey, you show some respect. This one here's not for smellin'.
jokes.setup: Inappropriate question from prisoner to Marge
jokes.punchline: Jack defending Marge's honor in his own way
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Jack as protective but crude

jokes.text: Hey, can I smell your clothes?
jokes.punchline: Now happening at chiropractor's office
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that doesn't add much to the original joke

jokes.text: Your spine is more twisted than Sinbad's take on marriage.
jokes.punchline: Comparing spine to comedian's material
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference but good structure comparing physical to comedic twists

jokes.text: Oh, I'd love to. But to be honest... modern medicine has a lousy record of treating the back. We spend too much time on the front.
jokes.setup: Homer wants drugs and surgery for back
jokes.punchline: Doctor's literal interpretation of medical priorities
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about medical focus with literal interpretation

jokes.text: Yeah, there's some neat stuff on the front.
jokes.setup: Doctor says medicine focuses on the front
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical literal and obvious observation

jokes.text: Well, that is our official stance. But between you, me and my golf clubs, they're miracle workers.
jokes.punchline: Doctor secretly admits they work, mentions golf clubs
jokes.explanation: Good satire of medical profession politics and doctor stereotypes

jokes.text: There. See how much better that feels? Yes. Much better.
jokes.punchline: Innuendo about brushing vs stabbing motions
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that works on surface level but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: Well, if you really gotta know, I shot a guy named Apu.
jokes.setup: Marge asks how someone so sensitive ended up in prison
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of shooting familiar character
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation connecting to established character

jokes.text: Well, you know, lots of people shoot Apu. It's just a hundred-dollar fine now.
jokes.punchline: Marge treats it as common occurrence with minimal penalty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Marge's casual acceptance of violence is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: 'A Time to Kill.' Titles are hard.
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent comment on creative difficulty
jokes.explanation: Good irony - ominous title treated as mere artistic challenge

jokes.text: Huh? Less yakkin', more crackin'!
jokes.setup: Dr. Steve wakes up sleeping Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's rhyming demand for chiropractic service
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer rhyme with characteristic impatience

jokes.text: Now, Homer, we don't actually crack backs. It's merely an adjustment. Okay, you're gonna hear a loud cracking sound.
jokes.setup: Doctor corrects Homer about not 'cracking' backs
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions the cracking sound
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction of professional terminology with reality

jokes.text: Now, I'll need to see you three times a week for, uh, many years.
jokes.setup: Homer's back feels better after one treatment
jokes.punchline: Doctor immediately suggests extensive ongoing treatment
jokes.explanation: Good satire of medical business practices

jokes.text: Bart, Sideshow Bob says he'll be seeing you real soon. [Chuckles] That Bob!
jokes.punchline: Treats death threat as friendly greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge obliviousness to obvious threat

jokes.text: I might grease the wheels of justice with some cookies.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if she'll bribe the parole board
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent description of bribery
jokes.explanation: Good Marge euphemism for corruption using wholesome metaphor

jokes.text: Not till you finish your sundae. Ohh! [Gulps, Gasps] Is that enough? Two more bites!
jokes.punchline: Marge forces him to finish dessert like a child
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of parent-child dynamics with Homer forced to eat

jokes.text: But I've seen this warden turn down brownies- honest to goodness brownies!
jokes.setup: Discussion of bribing warden with baked goods
jokes.punchline: Brownies presented as ultimate temptation he resisted
jokes.explanation: Treats brownies as serious corruption attempt, good escalation

jokes.text: someone with his talent... belongs on a boardwalk doing caricatures, not behind bars.
jokes.punchline: Her vision of his artistic future is quite modest
jokes.explanation: Great Marge moment - supportive but with limited artistic vision

jokes.text: Actually he was. He waited with me till the ambulance came then ran like a deer.
jokes.setup: Warden says Jack wasn't polite to his victim
jokes.punchline: Jack was actually considerate before fleeing
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - polite criminal behavior

jokes.text: Now, which way is Mecca? 'Cause I gotta do a little prayin'. Uh, Mecca? Well, let- Nah! [Laughing] I'm just yanking your chain! [Laughing] I'm Jewish.
jokes.setup: Jack asks about Islamic prayer direction
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was joking, he's actually Jewish
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jack's sense of humor

jokes.text: Not trash can, Son- Dr. Homer's Miracle Spine-O-Cylinder! Patent pending.
jokes.setup: Bart calls Homer's invention a trash can
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose name for garbage can
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer grandiosity applied to mundane object

jokes.text: perfectly match the contours of the human 'vertebrains.'
jokes.punchline: Combines 'vertebrae' and 'brains' incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Good Homer malapropism combining medical terms

jokes.text: You brought a convict to live here near my unpatented idea?
jokes.setup: Homer worried about Jack being a convict
jokes.punchline: His concern is protecting his trash can invention
jokes.explanation: Homer's skewed priorities - more worried about invention than safety

jokes.text: I seen your idea, and I don't want it. All I need is three squares and a job.
jokes.setup: Homer worried Jack will steal his idea
jokes.punchline: Jack dismisses the invention completely
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's ego about his invention

jokes.text: So, Homer, you think you can fix my sciatica? Hmm. I don't know what that is, so I'm gonna say yes.
jokes.setup: Patient asks about specific medical condition
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - ignorance as qualification

jokes.text: Now go limp. I'm limp. One, two, better not sue.
jokes.punchline: His rhyme acknowledges malpractice risk
jokes.explanation: Good rhyme that acknowledges the danger of Homer's treatment

jokes.text: My searing leg pain is now a gentle numbness.
jokes.punchline: Success defined as different type of pain
jokes.explanation: Good medical humor about dubious improvement

jokes.text: Boy, talk about irony. The A.M.A. Tries to drive you guys out of business. Now you're doing the same to me! Think about the irony.
jokes.punchline: Homer's obsession with pointing out the irony
jokes.explanation: Homer correctly identifies irony but won't stop explaining it

jokes.text: Not unless you think about the irony!
jokes.punchline: Homer's final demand about the irony
jokes.explanation: Continues the irony running gag but with diminishing returns

jokes.text: It's a small liberal arts college. Very law-abiding. No convicts at all.
jokes.punchline: Marge's elaborately specific lie
jokes.explanation: Marge forced to lie gets over-specific, revealing her discomfort

jokes.text: Ew! How could anyone consider that making love?
jokes.punchline: Skinner's horrified reaction to sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Skinner's prudish reaction to implied visual content

jokes.text: Actually, Marge, it's the third if you count that farm couple.
jokes.setup: Marge says this is his second chance
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals more victims casually
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation revealing Jack's criminal history

jokes.text: That is awesome. Finally, art that doesn't suck.
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt artistic appreciation
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart directness about preferring edgy art

jokes.text: Yeah. You see, I didn't want to go so cutesy-wootsy. Because- Because it's not my style.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to explain his artistic vision
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted and has to defend against 'cutesy-wootsy'
jokes.explanation: Artist vs. authority conflict with juvenile language

jokes.text: Don't forget I can send you back where you came from, college boy.
jokes.punchline: Uses 'college boy' thinking Jack went to college
jokes.explanation: Callback to Marge's lie about Jack being from college

jokes.text: Listen, if you're done with that washing machine, can I make some booze in it?
jokes.setup: Jack asking Marge about household items
jokes.punchline: Wants to use washing machine for illegal alcohol production
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's criminal mindset applied to domestic setting

jokes.text: Now I can focus on my crippling emotional pain. Oh, Daddy, Daddy, why? Why won't you hug me? You hugged the mailman!
jokes.punchline: Reveals deeper psychological trauma
jokes.explanation: Great Moe character work - trading physical for emotional pain

jokes.text: Ah, you saw our bus ad.
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud reference to cheap advertising
jokes.explanation: Homer's pride in his makeshift medical practice

jokes.text: Forget it, Homer. It's Chiro-town.
jokes.punchline: Chinatown parody about chiropractic conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of famous Chinatown quote applied to medical conspiracy

jokes.text: I almost thought he said a school Muriel. [Laughs] Muriel's his sister.
jokes.punchline: His own mishearing creates unfunny personal reference
jokes.explanation: Skinner's attempt at humor falls flat, which is the joke

jokes.text: Oh, thank you, Bruce Vilanch. Whoopi would've made it work.
jokes.punchline: References comedy writers and performers
jokes.explanation: Inside comedy reference that doesn't land strongly

jokes.text: No napkin could wipe the crumbs of failure from your mouth!
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor connecting napkin to failure
jokes.explanation: Good escalating metaphor tying back to napkin sketch reference

jokes.text: But years from now, people will point to these cuddly little creatures and say... 'That's a Jack Crowley.'
jokes.punchline: Marge's delusional prediction of artistic recognition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge optimism about clearly failed art

jokes.text: That's what you said about the stuffed peppers, and you lost the young males!
jokes.punchline: Treats lunch menu like serious marketing demographics
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic humor about cafeteria as business strategy

jokes.text: Dear Lord, I've peed in front of him!
jokes.punchline: Skinner's horrified realization about bathroom vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Great Skinner moment - priggish horror at perceived vulnerability

jokes.text: were it not stuffed with asbestos.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dangerous building materials
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional negligence treated casually

jokes.text: And you can't believe the foul language in that place.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he doesn't want to go back to prison
jokes.punchline: His biggest complaint is language, not violence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - misplaced priorities about prison problems

jokes.text: We don't have time for guessing games! Nah, let's try it. It might be fun.
jokes.setup: Marge has evidence of real arsonist
jokes.punchline: Wiggum prioritizes fun over solving crime
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum - treating police work like a game

jokes.text: Catch the fever, Skinner! [Cackling]
jokes.punchline: His maniacal celebration of school spirit through arson
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Skinner's repressed personality exploding

jokes.text: Marge, this is the God's truth. I burned the mural. But I did not burn Skinner's car.
jokes.punchline: Admits to one crime while denying the other
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - selective honesty that makes him look worse

jokes.text: Uh, i-is prison really like that? Wouldn't know. We only get basic cable.
jokes.setup: Cop asks about HBO prison show
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact response about cable limitations
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response deflating cop's curiosity about prison

jokes.text: Yeah, I also like that Sex in the City. Huh! None of those girls look like my wife. [Laughing]
jokes.punchline: Implies his wife doesn't look like attractive TV characters
jokes.explanation: Generic self-deprecating humor that doesn't add much

jokes.text: A lot of guys in prison say they're gay, but I don't know. They look strong to me.
jokes.punchline: Jack's confused logic about sexuality and strength
jokes.explanation: Jack's naive confusion about stereotypes, somewhat dated humor

tmdb_overview: Marge tries to rehabilitate a convict with remarkable artistic talent. Meanwhile, Homer uses a trash can to fix people's spines.
